name: Kauai
description_short: Kaua'i Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, NCUA-insured credit union established in 1947, serving the Kaua'i community with banking and lending products.
description_long:
Kaua'i Federal Credit Union was established in 1947 and has grown to serve the Kaua'i Island community for over 75 years. As a federally-insured credit union (NCUA), it operates as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution with a stated commitment to local economic resilience and community stewardship. The organization emphasizes its local roots with the motto 'Kauai Owns Us, Kauai Made Us, We're For Kauai.'

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products including checking and savings accounts (with youth account options for ages 0-17), auto loans, personal loans, real estate/mortgage financing, home equity lines of credit (HELOCs), and credit cards. Members receive 24/7 access to accounts through online banking, mobile app, and telephone banking. The institution operates a network of surcharge-free ATMs throughout Kaua'i to keep member funds local.

Kaua'i FCU distinguishes itself through its community-focused mission and local reinvestment philosophy. The organization actively supports island initiatives, hosts youth financial education programs during Youth Month, and partners with local organizations like the Little Miso Bookmobile. The credit union structure ensures profits are returned to members rather than shareholders, and membership is open to those with ties to the Kaua'i community.

As a regional credit union, Kaua'i FCU may have more limited product sophistication and fewer branch locations than large national banks, and its services appear primarily designed for local island residents. Members should verify eligibility requirements and current rate terms, as advertised rates (like the 4.99% APR HELOC introductory offer ending June 30th) are time-limited and subject to change.
services: Share savings accounts (with minimum $5 opening, $100 minimum for APY)
Pueo Accounts for youth ages 0-17 (requires joint adult account holder)
Youth Accounts for ages 10-17 (requires joint adult account holder)
Home equity lines of credit (HELOC) with 4.99% APR introductory rate for 24 months
pros: NCUA-insured member accounts with deposit protection up to $250,000
Surcharge-free ATM network keeps member funds circulating locally on Kaua'i
Youth accounts available for ages 0-17 with joint adult account holder requirement
Introductory HELOC rate of 4.99% APR for 24 months (limited time offer)
Comprehensive loan products including auto, personal, real estate, and HELOC financing
24/7 account access via online banking, mobile app, and telephone banking
Member referral program offering quarterly $100 prizes for bringing in new members
cons: Low savings account APY of only 0.10% requires $100 minimum balance to earn advertised rate
Limited geographic scope—services primarily available to Kaua'i island residents and members
Minimum $5 account opening requirement and limited information about account fees on homepage
No mention of competitive rates across all loan products or rate comparison tools
Limited online presence and product details compared to larger national credit unions
best_for: Kaua'i island residents seeking local community-focused banking and loan services
Parents wanting to teach youth (ages 0-17) healthy financial habits through youth savings accounts
Homeowners with equity seeking low introductory-rate HELOCs for home improvement or refinancing
Auto buyers and borrowers looking for personal loans with local reinvestment benefits
diagnosis: Kaua'i Federal Credit Union is best for Kaua'i island residents seeking community-focused banking with locally-reinvested funds, competitive loan products, and youth financial education programs. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—membership and full service access are restricted to those with ties to the Kaua'i community, and the credit union's smaller scale means fewer branches and potentially less product innovation compared to national institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Kauai — Credit Union in Lihue, HI | CreditDoc
meta_description: Kauai — NCUA-insured credit union in Lihue, HI. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
